task_completion.description: Verify the diagram title is set correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text element or title on the EdrawMax canvas containing the exact text 'Payment Processing Sequence Diagram'.

task_completion.description: Verify all six required participants are added in the correct order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for six UML lifeline boxes arranged left-to-right at the top of the diagram with the exact labels: 'User', 'Web Frontend', 'API Gateway', 'Payment Service', 'Fraud Detection', and 'Bank API'.

task_completion.description: Verify the synchronous (forward) messages are added between the correct participants.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for solid arrows pointing right with the labels: 'Submit Payment' (User to Web Frontend), 'POST /payments' (Web Frontend to API Gateway), 'processPayment()' (API Gateway to Payment Service), 'checkFraud()' (Payment Service to Fraud Detection), and 'authorizeTransaction()' (Payment Service to Bank API).

task_completion.description: Verify the return messages are added between the correct participants.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for dashed arrows pointing left with the labels: 'fraudResult()' (Fraud Detection to Payment Service), 'authorizationResponse()' (Bank API to Payment Service), 'paymentResult()' (Payment Service to API Gateway), 'HTTP 200 Response' (API Gateway to Web Frontend), and 'Payment Confirmation' (Web Frontend to User).

task_completion.description: Verify the diagram is saved as an EDDX file and exported as a PNG image to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for EdrawMax 'Save As' and 'Export' dialogs showing the file names 'payment_sequence_diagram.eddx' and 'payment_sequence_diagram.png' being saved to the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory, or a file manager window confirming their presence.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Wondershare EdrawMax graphical user interface to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the EdrawMax application window open with the diagram being actively edited on the canvas, rather than terminal windows or text editors.

integrity.description: Verify the diagram is constructed using actual UML elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: The canvas should show distinct, selectable UML shapes (lifelines, activation boxes, message arrows) rather than a single pasted image or plain text blocks.

integrity.description: Verify the files were created via the application's save/export features.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not use terminal commands like 'touch' or 'echo' to create empty or fake files at the target paths.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output paths are '/home/ga/Documents/payment_sequence_diagram.eddx' and '/home/ga/Documents/payment_sequence_diagram.png'. The diagram must contain exactly 6 participants: 'User', 'Web Frontend', 'API Gateway', 'Payment Service', 'Fraud Detection', and 'Bank API'. The diagram must contain exactly 10 messages: 'Submit Payment', 'POST /payments', 'processPayment', 'checkFraud', 'fraudResult', 'authorizeTransaction', 'authorizationResponse', 'paymentResult', 'HTTP 200 Response', and 'Payment Confirmation'.
